commit.message:
Add issue assignment strategy and GitHub Projects mandate to coordination protocols (#12)

No explicit mapping existed between issue type labels and agent
assignment, and there was no policy requiring trackable work to live in
a GitHub Project board.

### Issue Assignment Strategy
Added a triage lookup table under `Communication Conventions` in
`PROTOCOLS.md` mapping `type:*` label combinations to the responsible
agent and `owner:*` label:

| Label(s) | Agent | `owner:*` |
|---|---|---|
| `type:feature` + `type:backend` | dev-backend | `owner:dev-backend` |
| `type:feature` + `type:frontend` | dev-frontend | `owner:dev-frontend`
|
| `type:bug` | Owner of affected area | per area |
| `type:infra` | dev-infra | `owner:dev-infra` |
| `type:research` | biz-research | `owner:biz-research` |
| `type:cost` | cost-controller | `owner:cost-controller` |

Rules: one primary owner per issue (split if multi-agent), bugs assign
to area owner, unlabelled issues default to `owner:pm`.

Also extended the label table with four previously missing `type:*`
labels: `type:backend`, `type:frontend`, `type:infra`, `type:test`.

### GitHub Projects Mandate
Added a top-level `GitHub Projects Mandate` section enforcing that all
trackable items — issues, PRs, roadmap entries, effort estimates, sprint
goals — must be attached to a GitHub Project board. Key points:

- Both **PM** and **CEO** may create a project board when one doesn't
exist
- Default board template: Kanban with `Backlog / In Progress / In Review
/ Done`
- PM must add every issue/PR to the board at creation time; closing an
untracked issue requires retroactive addition
- Project board is the authoritative source of truth — progress must not
live only in Telegram or workspace files

- Hooks are host-agnostic (Claude Code, Amplifier, Copilot)

Extract AMD RDNA opcode constants (Step 7)

- Created rdna_opcodes module with 30 instruction opcode constants
  (VOP1: 8, VOP2: 11, VOP3: 2, SOP1: 2, SOP2: 4)
- Created bitfields module with 13 encoding masks and prefixes
- Refactored encode_vop1, encode_vop2, encode_vop3, encode_sop1,
  encode_sop2, encode_image_sample, and encode_export functions

Validation:
- grep -cE "0x[0-9a-fA-F]+" excluding constants: 1 (test only)
- Build: success
- Tests: all pass

Rationale: Bare hex literals in GPU instruction encoding make bugs
invisible. Named constants improve maintainability and reduce the risk
of typos when encoding RDNA instructions.

fix: handle slash commands immediately during active response (#43)

The stream isolation changes (channelIdleWaiters) caused ALL slash
commands to queue behind the active response, making /stop, /context,
/status etc. unresponsive until the agent finished.

Now handleMidTurnMessage dispatches commands in three tiers:
- Critical (/stop, /new): execute immediately, cancel active stream
- Safe (/context, /status, /help, /verbose, /autopilot, /mcp, /model,
  /reasoning, /streamer-mode): respond immediately without blocking
- Complex (/skills, /schedule, /rules, /reload, /resume, model/agent
  switch): defer to serialized path (need complex rendering or session ops)

markIdleImmediate is called AFTER cleanup completes (not before) to
prevent queued messages from racing with cancel/abort operations.
All mid-turn responses include threadRootId for thread-aware replies.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Development >> Master (Mar 9,2026) (#539)

This pull request introduces several improvements and fixes across the
codebase, focusing on permission handling, error logging, robustness in
field comment management, and file download functionality. The most
notable changes are grouped below:

### Permissions and Access Control

* Refactored `check_chart_permissions_and_settings` and
`check_card_permissions_and_settings` methods to accept an optional
`parent_dt_name` parameter, allowing for dashboard context-aware
permission checks. If the parent is a dashboard, users without report
permissions can still view charts/cards.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b31463e5509e8762327667139c1595cdb274a647f8e5f4c63066515edd15a43fL18-R26)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b31463e5509e8762327667139c1595cdb274a647f8e5f4c63066515edd15a43fL36-R41)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-b31463e5509e8762327667139c1595cdb274a647f8e5f4c63066515edd15a43fL109-R114)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-91421d7afaf3a01cee8c19677ea0f1220d084c58d498591e6dfdcd6a8ccb4fcdL19-R27)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-91421d7afaf3a01cee8c19677ea0f1220d084c58d498591e6dfdcd6a8ccb4fcdL37-R42)
[[6]](diffhunk://#diff-91421d7afaf3a01cee8c19677ea0f1220d084c58d498591e6dfdcd6a8ccb4fcdL60-R67)
* Improved permission logic for copying role permissions by replacing
legacy commented code with a simplified, active implementation.

### Robustness and Error Handling

* Enhanced error handling in `get_files` by adding try-except blocks and
logging for child document fetching, preventing silent failures when
retrieving related documents.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b66df0d206ddd3fd03cdd739bd1fee49329a1a56e6e165a3290045da4d89fa45L1331-R1170)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b66df0d206ddd3fd03cdd739bd1fee49329a1a56e6e165a3290045da4d89fa45L1409)
* Filtered out empty field names in field comment count calculations to
ensure only valid fields are counted.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b66df0d206ddd3fd03cdd739bd1fee49329a1a56e6e165a3290045da4d89fa45R842)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b66df0d206ddd3fd03cdd739bd1fee49329a1a56e6e165a3290045da4d89fa45L1078-R884)

### Field Comment Management

* Made `field_name`, `field_label`, and `comment_text` parameters
optional in `save_field_comment`, defaulting to empty strings and
improving robustness when saving or fetching comments.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b66df0d206ddd3fd03cdd739bd1fee49329a1a56e6e165a3290045da4d89fa45L538-R345)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b66df0d206ddd3fd03cdd739bd1fee49329a1a56e6e165a3290045da4d89fa45L581-R386)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-b66df0d206ddd3fd03cdd739bd1fee49329a1a56e6e165a3290045da4d89fa45L596-R402)
* Updated `DocType Field Comment` schema to remove the required
constraint from the `field_name` property.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-461b79ce3cd28e2ce2781a351b9ca334169ac21d60e561e5d600a3fea24b751eL38-R38)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-461b79ce3cd28e2ce2781a351b9ca334169ac21d60e561e5d600a3fea24b751eL63-R62)

### File Download Functionality

* Added a new API endpoint in `frappe_theme/apis/file_manager.py` for
downloading files, handling file streaming and response setup for client
downloads.
* Changed file download handling in the dynamic HTML component to open
files in a new browser tab instead of using a hidden anchor element.

### Miscellaneous

* Added a new variable `current_comment_frm` in `fields_comment.js` to
track which document the sidebar is currently showing.
* Improved workflow override logic by calling a custom
`after_custom_approval` method on documents if it exists, supporting
extensibility for workflow actions.
* Fixed a bug in the dynamic HTML component where the `doc` property was
accessed incorrectly.

Clarify skillpm’s position relative to GitHub CLI/Claude plugin specs (#57)

Users asked what emerging GitHub CLI and Claude plugin specs imply for
skillpm. This PR clarifies that those specs are host/runtime extension
mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

- **Documentation: plugin-spec positioning**
  - Added a new section to `README.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs (GitHub CLI / Claude Code)`
  - Added a matching section to `docs/index.md`:

commit.message:
feat: implement Google OAuth authentication UI (#7)

* feat: implement Google OAuth authentication UI

Implement complete frontend authentication system integrating with backend
Google OAuth API. Uses modern Google Identity Services for sign-in,
session-based authentication with HTTP-only cookies, and React Context for
state management.

Features:
- Google Identity Services integration (modern API, not deprecated gapi)
- Three UI states: loading, unauthenticated, authenticated
- Session-based authentication (cookies managed by backend)
- React Context (AuthProvider) for auth state management
- Comprehensive test suite with 97.44% coverage (15 tests)
- Full documentation of authentication flow and setup

Authentication Flow:
1. Startup: Check auth with GET /user/current
2. Login: User clicks Google button → Google provides ID token →
   POST /auth/login with Bearer token → Backend verifies and sets session cookie
3. Session: All API requests include credentials for cookies
4. Logout: POST /auth/logout clears session

Technical Details:
- Frontend never reads/parses cookies (HTTP-only)
- Backend is source of truth for authentication
- 401 responses clear frontend auth state
- Google ID token (credential) used, not OAuth access tokens
- No localStorage for auth (session cookies only)

WebSocket proxy:
- TCP proxy on configurable port (default 9224) routes to Chrome CDP
- Clients connect via ws://127.0.0.1:9224/<session_name>
- Bidirectional frame relay with automatic session lookup
- Enables external DevTools/Playwright to connect to daemon sessions

Session transfer:
- session_save: captures current URL, cookies, localStorage to JSON
- session_restore: navigates to saved URL and restores state
- Files stored in runtime_dir as session-<name>.json
- Survives daemon restarts when paired with session_create

Hot-reload config:
- SIGHUP handler reloads config.toml without daemon restart
- daemon_reload command for programmatic reload via socket
- Hot-reloadable: idle_timeout, max_sessions, shutdown_grace_secs
- Watch channel propagates idle timeout changes to timer task

workaround: avoid SDK cancellation serialization bug (#320)

## Summary

Pass \CancellationToken.None\ to SDK \SendAsync\ calls to prevent
\StreamJsonRpc\'s \StandardCancellationStrategy\ from attempting to
serialize \RequestId\, which is not included in the SDK's AOT-safe JSON
context.

Fixes #319

## Root Cause (SDK Bug)

When a \CancellationToken\ fires during an SDK call:
1. \StreamJsonRpc.StandardCancellationStrategy.CancelOutboundRequest()\
tries to send a \$/cancelRequest\ notification
2. This requires serializing \StreamJsonRpc.RequestId\
3. The SDK's \SystemTextJsonFormatter\ doesn't include \RequestId\ in
its \TypeInfoResolverChain\
4. \NotSupportedException\ is thrown, becomes unobserved, kills the
JSON-RPC reader loop silently
5. Session gets stuck — no events arrive, watchdog eventually fires

## Why This Workaround Works

PolyPilot already handles cancellation at the TCS level:
- \AbortSessionAsync\ calls \ResponseCompletion.TrySetCanceled()\
- Watchdog timeout clears \IsProcessing\ state
- All abort/error paths properly clean up state

The \CancellationToken\ was only used by SDK to send server-side
cancellation notifications — which is the broken path.

## Trade-off

The server won't receive \$/cancelRequest\ notifications, so it may
continue processing cancelled requests. However:
- Local state is correctly cleaned up (good UX)
- Watchdog provides safety net
- Server resources are wasted but operations complete gracefully

## Investigation Summary

4 parallel sub-agents analyzed:

| Agent | Focus | Finding |
|-------|-------|---------|
| SDK changelogs | Breaking changes | None documented for cancellation |
| SDK Client.cs | JsonRpc config | Confirms
\StandardCancellationStrategy\ used, \RequestId\ missing from JSON
context |
| PolyPilot audit | CancellationToken usage | Our usage is correct |
| SDK samples | Recommended patterns | Don't use CancellationToken at
all |

**All agents agree**: This is an SDK bug, not a PolyPilot defect.

## Changes

- **CopilotService.cs**: 2 lines changed
  - Primary \SendAsync\ call (line 2351)
  - Reconnect retry \SendAsync\ call (line 2499)

## Testing

- Build passes
- 139 watchdog tests pass
- Manual testing: orchestrator sessions no longer get stuck on
cancellation

## SDK Versions Affected

- Broken: 0.1.30+ (after AOT/trimming changes)
- Works: 0.1.26

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace reference casts with `addr_of!` in `Soul::value_ptr` and `Soul::count_ptr` (#25)

* Initial plan

* fix(soul): replace reference casts with addr_of! in value_ptr and count_ptr

Use `core::ptr::addr_of!` to obtain raw pointers to Soul fields without
forming intermediate shared references, as recommended by the Rust Nomicon.
This avoids a provenance mismatch where a `*mut T` was derived from `&T`.

Fixes issue 17.

Co-authored-by: Magicolo <5423040+Magicolo@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use addr_of on self fields and simplify casts

Replace addr_of!((*self.get_ref()).field) with addr_of!(self.field)
and rely on type inference for NonNull::new_unchecked casts. Reflow a
long safety doc comment and tidy ptr imports.
